description: Representative observability event categories for GrantLayer structured logging and observability readiness baseline. This is a design artifact; no observability or logging is implemented.

eventTypeDefinitions.description: HTTP/API request start and completion; method, path, status, latency.
Unhandled or structured API errors; status code, error code, error message.
Login, logout, identity verification attempts, and outcomes.
Authorization checks, granted or denied, with policy context.
Evidence submitted for verification and the result.
Grant state changes, who initiated them, and the resulting state.
Policy rules evaluated during a request or workflow step.
Database reads and writes, success or failure, not the raw query.
Runtime configuration changes, reloads, or validation results.
Operator-initiated commands, configuration changes, or recoveries.

operational_log.description: System health, performance, and operator incident response signals. Short retention. Operator and SRE access.

product_audit_record.description: Permanent, append-only record of decisions and state changes. Long-term retention. Auditor and compliance access.

provenance_record.description: Cryptographically linked chain of custody for grant decisions. Immutable storage. Auditor and provenance system access.

security_event.description: Detection and forensic analysis of security-relevant activity. Long retention. Security team and SIEM access.

debug_trace.description: Developer troubleshooting output. Very short retention or disabled. Developer-only access. Never enabled in production.

globalRules: Replace redacted fields with standard marker [REDACTED] or structured equivalent.
Do not truncate or partially mask in a way that leaks information.
Apply redaction before serialization to the log sink.
Log redaction failures as a separate configuration_event or security_event.

low.description: Minimal redaction; generally safe to log.

medium.description: Some fields may need redaction or omission.

high.description: Strict redaction; evidence, credentials, and personal data must be hashed or omitted.
